text: Ring roller coaster three is the final ring roller coaster exercise in our curriculum at Lahey. It follows the same format and rules as ring roller coaster one and two, but provides a even more difficult course that the rings must travel around the wire.
The right hand is used to grab the ring in the anterior portion with the hand pronated.

In the upper left portion of the screen, the surgeon's hand is visible, using the tips of their fingers to manipulate the da Vinci hand controls, maximizing range of motion while navigating the course.

The camera view is kept close and centered in the working field to minimize small collisions on the wire during the ring roller coaster exercise. Frequent camera adjustments are visible to prevent additional collisions.

When experiencing a collision, which could cause a penalty, trying to correct for that is much easier when you're on a more straight or linear path versus on one of the curves or angles of the wire course.
You'll see that as we come to this two right angle turns, we have a collision, but again, correct it once we get over that turn.
The surgeon navigates two curves using the da Vinci system, requiring a full turn of the hand with wrist articulation visible in the hand controls.
The video shows a few collisions occurring during the navigation of the wire course, indicating that the camera position might need adjustment for better visibility and control.
Success on this exercise entails using the tips of your fingers on the hand controls, maximizing the full range of motion with the wrist articulation and good camera coordination.
